ddls:
CREATE TABLE "articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each article */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the article */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the article */,
  "corpus" TEXT /* The full corpus or text of the article */,
  "transform_text" TEXT /* Transformed text for analysis */,
  "word_counts" INTEGER /* Total word count in the article */,
  "average_sentence_length" REAL /* Average number of words per sentence */,
  "char_count" INTEGER /* Total character count in the article */,
  "average_word_length" REAL /* Average length of words in the article */,
  "syllable_count" INTEGER /* Total syllable count in the article */,
  "author" TEXT /* Name of the author */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date of publication */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the article (e.g., blog, news site) */,
  "is_original" INTEGER /* Indicates if the article is original or a repost */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the article (e.g., English, Spanish) */,
  "read_time_minutes" INTEGER /* Estimated read time in minutes */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of tags for the article */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the article being measured */,
  "complex_count" INTEGER /* Count of complex words in the article */,
  "sentence_length" INTEGER /* Total sentence count in the article */,
  "fog_index" REAL /* Fog index to evaluate readability */,
  "subjectivity" REAL /* Subjectivity score of the article */,
  "flesch_reading_ease" REAL /* Flesch Reading Ease score */,
  "flesch_kincaid_grade_level" REAL /* Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level */,
  "positive_sentiment_score" REAL /* Positive sentiment score */,
  "negative_sentiment_score" REAL /* Negative sentiment score */,
  "neutral_sentiment_score" REAL /* Neutral sentiment score */,
  "emotional_intensity" REAL /* Overall emotional intensity score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "personal_pronouns" (
  "pronoun_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the pronoun record */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the article containing the pronouns */,
  "pronouns_used" TEXT /* List of personal pronouns identified in the article */,
  "total_pronoun_count" INTEGER /* Total count of personal pronouns */,
  "first_person_count" INTEGER /* Count of first-person pronouns */,
  "second_person_count" INTEGER /* Count of second-person pronouns */,
  "third_person_count" INTEGER /* Count of third-person pronouns */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pronoun_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_personal_pronouns_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, general user) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL or path to the user's profile picture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the article */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the article was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_categories" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the notification is sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Notification message */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the setting belongs */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the setting was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
